questions: php vs node.js which is better for backend?
what can you build with php?
answers: PHP is a server-side scripting language that powers over 75% of the web including WordPress, Facebook, and Wikipedia. Modern PHP (8.x) is fast, typed, and nothing like the PHP of old.
PHP is a widely-used backend language embedded in HTML. With frameworks like Laravel and Symfony, it's a mature ecosystem for building web applications, APIs, and content management systems.
PHP has evolved massively with version 8.x bringing JIT compilation, named arguments, attributes, and union types. It remains one of the most practical languages for web development.
Despite jokes about it, PHP is alive and thriving. Laravel is one of the most popular web frameworks, and PHP 8's performance improvements put it on par with many modern alternatives.
